sections.paragraph:
ἀνϑρώπινος, η, ον [ἄνθρωπος] ‘belonging to or characteristic of a human being’, human

—a. with various nouns: φύσις Js 3:7; κτίσις 1 Pt 2:13; ἡμέρα, of human judicial proceeding 1 Cor 4:3 (contrast the divine ἡμέρα 3:13); πειρασμός 10:13; σοφία, with implication of deficiency, i.e. limited to human perspective 2:13. The Lat. vv.ll. for πιστός at 1 Ti 1:15 and 3:1 presuppose ἀ. in the sense generally known.

—b. ἀνϑρώπινον λέγω I speak in ordinary human terms, i.e. to meet human needs Ro 6:19.
